name: Copperhead Homestead
description: I’m a seventh generation family farmer, raised on my daddy’s farm in sunny south Georgia. It is my passion to raise and sell food the way my grandparents (and great-grandparents!) did. Raising trustworthy food without chemicals, medications, or any factory farming techniques. Healthy, pastured cattle and hand planted (by me personally) heirloom produce, never coated with pesticides or herbicides. My dream is to bring back the golden days of farming: when farmers could feed their food to their own children, when neighbors could trust their local farmers to provide safe and responsible nutrition. Unfortunately, in these times, many food safety terms have become simply buzzwords–“free range,” “grass fed,” “antibiotic free” are no longer regulated terms. Stores are no longer required to state the country of origin on their meat and produce. I believe the only way to ensure your safety is to form a relationship with your local farmer; I want to be that farmer for you. If I am not, I would still like to help you find someone to fill your family’s needs.

name: Covey Rise Farms
description: We are a first generation farm owned and run by Charlie and Kerissa Payne. We utilize non-gmo feeds, rotational grazing, along with natural practices to produce wonderful meats for your family to enjoy. All products can be purchased through our website for local delivery or shipping nationwide!
address1: 7460 Fulton Creek Rd, Radnor, Ohio 43066, USA

name: Firefly Fields Farm
description:
Hi there, we are Christopher and Monica Moyer of Firefly Fields Farm, and we raise some of the best grass fed and grass finished beef that nature has to offer. Our rolling pastures boast a rich variety of rich forages and legumes and are home to millions of fireflies as well as a few cows. We raise foundation bred registered black Angus, as well as Angus /Aberdeen crosses. We rotationally graze them across our pastures so that we can keep fresh forage available at all times and give the land a chance to rest too. Our cows are happy cows here at our farm. To keep us, our land and our cattle healthy, we do not use any chemical fertilizers, pesticides, or other toxic applications whatsoever. You can know you are eating good food when you stock up your freezer with beef from Firefly Fields.

Any questions? Call Monica at 606-235-1473. Come, have a cup of tea and go check out the cows with me.

name: Meadowdale Farm and Sawmill
description:
At Meadowdale Farm, we are a family-owned and veteran-owned business passionate about raising the highest quality pasture-based meats and eggs since 1999. We raise our chickens and turkeys in small batches using grain sourced from nearby Georgia.

We love working with Farm to Table restaurants, caterers, as well as with customers that are looking for delicious, flavorful, nutrient dense poultry.

We prioritize sustainability and animal welfare in all our practices. We do not use herbicides in our fields, ensuring a natural and healthy environment for our animals and produce. We offer a variety of poultry products, including whole chickens and parts like thighs, boneless breasts, and wings. For pet owners, we also provide hard-to-find ingredients such as raw chicken feet and organs.

In addition to poultry, we offer sawmilling services for custom timber frames, historic renovations, and lumber. We sell natural bark mulch for gardens and garden compost, supporting your gardening needs. Our farm also provides homesteading, woodlot management, and sawmill consultations, sharing our expertise with others.

Recently, we relocated our farm to Tennessee, where we are blessed with an additional 90 days of grazing season! As our farm regrows, we will be reintroducing grass-fed lamb, forest-raised pork, and grass-fed beef into our offerings.

We are dedicated to reviving America's local food systems, where animal welfare, nutrient density, and soil health are of the utmost importance.

Our poultry is USDA inspected, and we are proud members of the American Pastured Poultry Producers Association, the Farmer Veteran Coalition, and our county TN Farm Bureau.

Connect with us on Instagram, Facebook, or our website to learn more and stay updated on our latest offerings and activities.

Please call/email us to set up a time for you to come visit and pick up some meat!
address1: 1400 Buttermilk Road, Lenoir City, Tennessee 37771, United States

name: The Log Cabin Ranch
description: The Log Cabin Ranch is a small family farm in southern Illinois where we strive to earn your trust and to provide you with convenient access to premium, nutrient rich, and healthy grass fed and finished beef and pasture raised pork that your family can enjoy with confidence. If you would like to learn more about our farm sign up for our email.
address1: 1384 North Log Cabin Lane, Ina, Illinois 62846, United States

name: Alpine Forest Farm
description: We are a veteran owned (2 x Air Force) transitioning organic farm. We raise chickens and pigs outdoors all spring, summer, and fall. In the winter our animals are in spacious quarters on deep bedding to stay clean, dry, and warm.
address1: 7101 Sparr Road, Gaylord, Michigan 49735, United States

name: Beery Farms of Michigan LLC
description:
Beery Farms of Michigan LLC is an 80-acre farm on which I raise all-grass-fed beef and goats.

The farm is operated in the Argentine grazing style using a diverse array of perennial grasses and summer-winter annuals to meet the year-round forage needs of the animals. Like the Argentines, the paddocks are divided to provide both annual and perennial forages simultaneously for an always fresh high quality diet. By using stockpiling in the fall and early greening small grains in the spring, it is only necessary to feed hay and haylage during the most severe winter months.

The farm is in the third year transition to Organic Certification. It is located just south of Lansing and visitors are always welcome.

About 100 acres for farm, 75 tillable, all is in permanent pasture which is cool season perrenials. Crossbred herd for beef and goat. Easy access off major highways. Welcome farm touring to see animals. Organic practices since 2004.

Beery Farms, Growers and Purveyors on Only Things Grass Fed.
address1: 849 N Aurelius Rd, Mason, MI 48854, USA

name: Casey Redtail Ranch
description:
The Casey family ranching tradition on the central coast of California spans more than a century of land and livestock stewardship.

As a young Irish emigrant, Jeremiah Casey arrived in Southern Monterey County in 1861 and started homesteading and ranching. He raised beef and finished them on grass and marketed them as best he could, given the limited resources of the day.

We continue the same style of beef husbandry today.

Cattle are raised in a natural, free range environment, in beautiful southern Monterey County, California. They are never given growth hormones, antibiotics or subjected to pesticides or herbicides. They are never fed grain and are always humanely treated.
address1: 53060 Pine Canyon Road, King City, California 93930, United States

name: Clarke Family Farms
description: Clarke Family Farms is a regenerative agriculture farm where all of our livestock is pasture-raised with non-GMO feed and USDA approved processing.
address1: 8796 West San Pierre Road, San Pierre, Indiana 46374, United States

name: Follow Me Land & Livestock
description:
Thank you for stopping in!
Allow us to introduce ourselves. We are Kevin and Laura Cory and our family includes five beautiful children. Kevin's service in the United State Army has allowed us the wonderful opportunity to see and experience many aspects of this Nation as well as our neighboring countries. We have traveled and lived all across the continent of North America from the frigid arctic north slopes of Alaska to the sunny tropics of Central America. Our farm is located in the Columbus, Georgia and Phenix City, Alabama area where we now live and raise our family. We are a family owned and operated regenerative livestock operation which uses a diverse herd of livestock classes to elicit the desired outcome on the land. We aim to maximize the animals' inherent instincts to provide to you a healthy protein source with integrity. You will share in the same quality we provide for our children. Not only do we raise our animals with integrity and try to allow them to simply be the animals God created them to be, our methods support the natural growth process of the earth itself. Through our diverse herd of tightly controlled and intensively managed livestock through rotational grazing, we can provide you as a land owner the opportunity to minimize your management costs by grazing the brush and understory of your personal land investment. Working with FMLLM will eliminate the costs of herbicides, pesticides, burning, and mowing while promoting fertility for your local population of deer and turkey. Simply put - we love helping others get their land back to green pastures and forests by using God's own animal creation and without the use of unnecessary, man made additives that can be harmful to all aspects of our environment and to your health.

name: JX Ranch
description: We raise and sell free-range, all-natural grass-fed and grass-finished beef, harvested at a relatively young age, and dry-aged 14-21 days for optimum flavor and tenderness. We manage our ranch using regenerative methods (holistic range management principles - rotational grazing with short grazing periods and long rest periods). We deliver to various locations throughout New Mexico, and ship retail cuts and 10 - 25 lb Beef Packs throughout the U.S. In the spring and fall we offer quarter, half and whole beef orders with free delivery to Alamogordo, Las Vegas, Santa Fe and Edgewood, New Mexico. We welcome visitors. Please call before coming out to make sure we're here! Mimi and Tom

name: Lick Skillet Farm
description:
Lick Skillet Farm is a Tennessee Century Farm offering forage-finished beef and pastured heritage pork.

We provide holistic management, regenerative land practices, and compassionate husbandry from birth to slaughter with no hormones, antibiotics, GMOs, pesticides, synthetic fertilizers, and no broadcast herbicides.

Our focus is on science-based policies for soil health, biological diversity, carbon sequestration, pollinator support, and maintaining and improving local ecosystem integrity, along with humane treatment and respect/dignity of animals we raise. Animal well-being translates into an awesome taste sensation, as well as healthier protein for your family.

Tennessee Century farm; focus on soil health, compassionate husbandry, quality products, carbon sequestration. Farm Stand open from 1-6 daily. Farm tours Sundays 1-3. order online for home delivery.

Order online for home delivery, or pick up at the farm.
address1: 800 Ken Manley Rd, New Market, TN 37820, USA

name: McGreal Family Farm
description:
McGreal Family Farm is a small farming business rooted in Clayton County, Northeast Iowa. Currently managed by a third generation Iowa farmer and his partner, this land is home.

Firmly planted in a small town, we believe in local connections but we also service areas as far away as Chicago with our beef. We are not shipping at this time but we can provide a delivery service. We specialize in pasture-raised, 100% grass-fed, custom beef, although we have big plans to grow flowers and vegetables during the next growing season. Deeply inspired by Barbara Kingsolver’s book Animal, Vegetable, Miracle, we implement organic practices on our farm without an official certification.

Our cattle are rotated often, allowing them to always have access to the most nutritious plants, and allowing the native flora to replenish naturally. Our Black Angus and Hereford Angus crossbreeds graze on 1,000 acres of driftless land composed of fields and timber. Our nearby creeks are fenced off to prevent unnecessary impact. Our beef is unadulterated, meaning they are hormone and antibiotic-free. Always.

Our cattle will become available for purchase January 2019, and we are currently accepting reservations for whole, halves and quarters. Contact us at info@mcgrealfamilyfarm.com or give us a call at 563-245-1041 with any questions or orders. We are located at 20447 Elm Road Strawberry Point, IA 52076. You can find us on Google Maps and Facebook under “McGreal Family Farm.” And, if you’re in the area, feel free to stop by.
address1: 20447 Elm Rd, Strawberry Point, IA 52076, USA

name: T-House Farms
description:
Hello! We are a small family farm in northeast Kansas. This piece land was purchased from our maternal grandparents, so it holds lots of wonderful memories. We are very interested in teaching our kids the value of taking care of the soil, work ethic, knowing where your food comes from, and increasing the quality of meat we consume. “You are what you eat.”

Our favorite product to tote is the grass-fed lamb due to the endless variety of possibilities available. Each member of the family has their fan favorites: Grilled lamb burgers, lamb fajitas with lamb shoulder, lamb shank stew, the lamb lollipops, and holiday leg of lamb. 🍔

We’ve kept bees since 2014 (when we lived in town and wanted to understand the best way to take care of fruit trees-save the pollinators!) We have enjoyed learning something new about them each season. 🍯

Our youngest son has us taking on the endeavor of milking cows. We’ve always had an affinity and love of other local family farm’s fresh milk. But due to driving distance when we moved to our own family farm we had to give up this local luxury. 🥛

What started as a way for us to enjoy fresh, quality meat chicken, grew into a twice a year butchering “mark your calendar” event with family, friends and neighbors. Due to our rotational grazing habits and our value of raising quality meat -we love raising and enjoying the fruits of our labor with pastured meat chickens. 🍗 Chicken noodle soup and roasted chicken with veggies are staples at our dinner table during the winter months. Please let us know ahead of the spring/fall season to put your name on the list as the pastured chicken sells out quickly.
address1: 3307 Dewberry Road, Morrill, Kansas 66515, United States

name: Taylor Family Farm
description:
Nestled in beautiful middle Tennessee, we raise 100% non-gmo, grass-fed beef, pastured chicken, eggs, and pork on our cozy family farm. We believe in healthy food, healthy people and healthy community. That’s why we’ve chosen to farm and feed our local neighbors, and surrounding communities, including you! Everything we grow is raised with love and care to ensure the highest quality and nutrition for our customers. All ration fed animals are fed our 100% non-gmo feed milled right here on the farm. You can shop online or visit our farm store, we’re open Tue-Sat 8 am – 5 pm. Closed all major holidays.

-See ya soon!
